title: Node naming and node term type
purpose: BEAM distribution requires every node to have a unique name (e.g., 'worker@host.local') and every PID/reference to carry a node origin. Currently, PIDs are plain u64 process IDs with no node context. This brief adds the Node concept — a named identity for the VM instance — and extends PID and Reference terms to carry their originating node. Without node-qualified identifiers, remote operations cannot distinguish local from remote processes.
task: Define the Node type, add a node registry, extend PID and Reference terms with node information, and implement the erlang:node/0 and erlang:node/1 BIFs.

requirements.title: Node type and local node identity
requirements.spec: Define a Node type representing a named BEAM node. The local node is set at VM startup and is immutable for the lifetime of the instance.
acceptance: Node struct with name: Atom and creation: u32 (distinguishes restarts of the same node name)
Scheduler/SharedState gains local_node: Node field, set at startup
Node::is_local(other: &Node) -> bool compares name and creation
Default local node is 'nonode@nohost' (matching BEAM's non-distributed mode)
Test: create VM with node name, verify node identity

requirements.title: Node-qualified PID terms
requirements.spec: Extend PID terms to carry their originating node. Local PIDs continue to use the compact immediate representation. Remote PIDs are boxed terms containing the node, process ID, and serial.
acceptance: Local PIDs remain immediate terms (no heap allocation, backward compatible)
Remote PID is a boxed term: [header] [node_atom] [pid_number] [serial]
BoxedTag::ExternalPid added (or similar)
Term::is_local_pid() and Term::is_remote_pid() discriminators
BinaryRef-style PidRef accessor handles both local and remote transparently
Test: local PID has no node overhead, remote PID carries node info

requirements.title: erlang:node/0 and erlang:node/1 BIFs
requirements.spec: Register node/0 (returns the local node name atom) and node/1 (returns the node of a PID, reference, or port term).
acceptance: node() returns the local node name atom
node(LocalPid) returns the local node name
node(RemotePid) returns the remote node name
node(non_pid) returns badarg
Test: node() returns 'nonode@nohost' in non-distributed mode

requirements.title: Node-qualified references
requirements.spec: Extend Reference terms to carry their originating node, same pattern as PIDs.
acceptance: Local references remain compact (backward compatible)
Remote references carry node information
erlang:node/1 works on references
Test: make_ref() produces a local reference, node(Ref) returns local node

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement network connections or handshake — that's B-114/B-115
SHALL NOT implement EPMD or name resolution — that's B-113
SHALL NOT change the local PID immediate representation — only add remote PID support
SHALL support non-distributed mode ('nonode@nohost') as the default
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
Existing PID tests pass unchanged (local PIDs unmodified)
